message:
fix(security): harden extensions against path traversal, command obfuscation, and OOM

Comprehensive security audit fixes across the extension system:

Path traversal prevention:
- safe_canonicalize() now handles non-existent paths via logical
  normalization with symlink-aware ancestor resolution, closing a gap
  where crafted paths could escape the extension root.
- New normalize_dot_segments() provides path normalization without
  filesystem I/O for pre-validation.
- strip_unc_prefix() handles Windows UNC path edge cases.
- PiJsResolver::resolve_module_path() now takes extension roots and
  enforces scope monotonicity: resolved paths (including symlink
  targets) must remain within the declared extension root.

Command obfuscation normalization:
- New normalize_command_for_classification() strips shell obfuscation
  techniques (\${ifs}, \$ifs, backslash-escaped whitespace) before
  dangerous command classification, preventing bypass of exec mediation.
- classify_dangerous_command() now detects "rm -rf /." as RecursiveDelete.
- classify_pipe_to_shell() enhanced for download-exec pipeline patterns.

Resource limits to prevent OOM:
- hash_canonical_json() and hash_canonical_shape() add depth limit (128)
  to prevent stack overflow on deeply nested JSON payloads.
- MAX_MODULE_SOURCE_BYTES (1GB) prevents loading oversized modules.
- MAX_JOBS_PER_TICK (10,000) caps hostcall batch processing per tick.
- compile_module_source() checks file metadata before reading.

Extension dispatcher improvements:
- Fast-lane dispatch now runs policy checks before executing hostcalls
  (was missing, allowing fast-lane to bypass capability policy).
- Log hostcalls now emit structured tracing instead of being silently
  swallowed in the fast-lane path.
- hostcall_io_hint() now classifies "edit" as IoHeavy and "bash" as
  CpuBound for better scheduling decisions.

Atomic file permission hardening:
- auth.rs: AuthStorage save uses OpenOptions::mode(0o600) instead of
  post-hoc set_permissions(), eliminating TOCTOU race window.
- anthropic.rs: kimi_device_id file creation uses same pattern.

Other fixes:
- strip_js_comments(): add ')' and ']' to bracket matching (parser bug).
- generate_proxy_stub_module(): fix format string interpolation.
- compute_canary_bucket(): use u16 instead of u8 to reduce modulo bias.
- check_exec_capability(): new sync helper for exec policy checking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
